items.title: MeitY Notifies Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming (PROG) Rules, 2026
items.whyInNews: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) notified the Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming (PROG) Rules, 2026 on 24 April 2026, which will come into effect from 1 May 2026 across India.
items.summary: The PROG Rules, 2026 have been framed under the PROG Act, 2025 to establish a structured regulatory framework for India’s online gaming sector. The rules introduce the Online Gaming Authority of India (OGAI) headquartered in New Delhi and define regulations for online money games, e-sports recognition, and user safety. They aim to curb illegal financial activities, ensure user protection, and promote responsible gaming while supporting industry growth.
keyPoints: PROG Rules, 2026 notified on 24 April 2026; effective from 1 May 2026
Online Gaming Authority of India (OGAI) established under MeitY with headquarters in New Delhi
Determination of online money games to be completed within 90 days based on stakes and monetisation
Registration certificate for online games valid up to 10 years; money games not eligible for e-sports recognition
Two-tier grievance redressal system with 30-day resolution at each stage and digital enforcement within 90 days
importantPoints: OGAI functions as an attached office of MeitY and operates digitally
Framed under the Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming (PROG) Act, 2025
Part of India’s push to regulate digital economy and promote e-sports ecosystem
Focus on user protection including age verification, parental controls, and addiction safeguards
Helps India position itself as a global hub for e-sports and digital innovation
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC General Awareness under Polity and Governance, especially regulatory bodies, digital laws, and emerging sectors like online gaming and e-sports

items.title: INS Nireekshak Deployed to Colombo for India–Sri Lanka DIVEX 2026
items.whyInNews: The Indian Navy deployed INS Nireekshak to Colombo in April 2026 to participate in the fourth edition of the India–Sri Lanka Diving Exercise (DIVEX 2026), strengthening maritime cooperation in the Indian Ocean Region.
items.summary: INS Nireekshak is participating in DIVEX 2026 in Colombo to enhance underwater operational coordination and interoperability between the Indian Navy and Sri Lankan Navy. The mission also includes humanitarian outreach through the Aarogya Maitri programme with the deployment of BHISM medical cubes. The exercise reflects India’s MAHASAGAR vision and 'Neighbourhood First' policy while reinforcing bilateral defence ties.
keyPoints: INS Nireekshak deployed to Colombo for 4th India–Sri Lanka Diving Exercise (DIVEX 2026)
Exercise focuses on underwater operations, interoperability, and maritime security cooperation
BHISM medical cubes deployed under Aarogya Maitri programme for HADR support
INS Nireekshak built in 1985 by Mazagon Shipbuilders; holds record for 257-metre deep dive
India–Sri Lanka signed 5-year defence MoU in April 2025 for strategic cooperation
importantPoints: INS Nireekshak is a Dive Support and Submarine Rescue Vessel of the Indian Navy
MAHASAGAR stands for Mutual and Holistic Advancement for Security and Growth Across Regions
Part of India’s ‘Neighbourhood First’ foreign policy initiative
BHISM cubes can handle up to 200 emergency cases autonomously
Regular exercises include SLINEX (Navy) and MITRA SHAKTI (Army)
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC General Awareness under Defence and International Relations, especially naval exercises, India’s maritime strategy, and bilateral defence cooperation

items.title: General Upendra Dwivedi Inducted into US Army War College International Hall of Fame
items.whyInNews: On 24 April 2026, Chief of Army Staff General Upendra Dwivedi was inducted into the International Hall of Fame at the US Army War College in Carlisle, Pennsylvania, becoming the third Indian Army chief to receive this honour.
items.summary: General Upendra Dwivedi was honoured at the US Army War College for his contributions to national security and global military cooperation during his official visit to the United States. He joined former Army Chiefs General V.K. Singh and General Bikram Singh as the only Indians to receive this distinction. The recognition reflects strengthening India–US defence ties, particularly in the Indo-Pacific region.
keyPoints: General Upendra Dwivedi inducted into International Hall of Fame at US Army War College, Pennsylvania
Became third Indian Army Chief after General V.K. Singh and General Bikram Singh to receive honour
US Army War College located in Carlisle, Pennsylvania and trains senior military leaders
Held discussions with US General Ronald P. Clark on Indo-Pacific security cooperation
Visit included academic lectures on leadership and global security challenges
importantPoints: General Upendra Dwivedi is the current Chief of Army Staff of India
International Hall of Fame recognises distinguished global military alumni
India–US defence cooperation has expanded significantly in the Indo-Pacific region
US Army War College is a premier institution for strategic military education
Reflects India’s rising strategic importance in global security architecture
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC General Awareness under Defence and International Relations, especially military honours, institutions, and India–US strategic cooperation

items.title: India’s First Integrated Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling Facility Set Up in Sikandrabad, Uttar Pradesh
items.whyInNews: In April 2026, Uttar Pradesh inaugurated India’s first integrated recycling facility for lithium-ion batteries and rare earth magnets at UPSIDC Industrial Area, Sikandrabad, developed by Rocklink India.
items.summary: The facility in Sikandrabad, Uttar Pradesh, is designed to recycle lithium-ion batteries, rare earth magnets, and industrial metal waste to support India’s clean energy transition. It has an initial battery recycling capacity of 10,000 tonnes per year and processes 60 tonnes of rare earth magnets monthly. The plant follows Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) norms and uses advanced technology to recover over 98% of key metals.
keyPoints: India’s first integrated lithium-ion battery recycling plant established in Sikandrabad, Uttar Pradesh
Developed by Rocklink India at UPSIDC Industrial Area
Initial capacity of 10,000 tonnes per year for lithium-ion battery recycling
Processes 60 tonnes of rare earth magnets per month and plans 1,500 tonnes annual rare-earth chloride unit by 2026
Achieves over 98% recovery of metals like aluminium, copper, and iron using R2 recycling process
importantPoints: Facility operates under Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) framework
Supports circular economy and sustainable resource management
Critical for electric vehicles, renewable energy, and advanced manufacturing sectors
Helps reduce dependence on imported critical minerals
Strengthens India’s clean energy and green technology ecosystem
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC General Awareness under Economy and Environment, especially topics like circular economy, recycling technologies, EV ecosystem, and government sustainability initiatives

items.title: UNGA President Annalena Baerbock to Visit India for Multilateral Talks
items.whyInNews: In April 2026, United Nations General Assembly President Annalena Baerbock announced her upcoming visit to India next week to strengthen multilateral cooperation and discuss global governance issues.
items.summary: UNGA President Annalena Baerbock will visit India to hold bilateral meetings with senior Indian officials on issues such as global governance reforms, climate action, and sustainable development. She will also interact with the UN Country Team in India led by Resident Coordinator Stefan Priesner. The visit highlights India’s growing role in global diplomacy and the United Nations system.
keyPoints: UNGA President Annalena Baerbock to visit India in April 2026
Discussions to focus on global governance reforms, SDGs, climate action, and peacebuilding
Will meet UN Country Team in India led by Resident Coordinator Stefan Priesner
UN General Assembly is one of the six principal organs of the United Nations
UNGA President is elected for a one-year term with each member country having one vote
importantPoints: Annalena Baerbock is President of the United Nations General Assembly
UN General Assembly plays a central role in global policy discussions
India advocates reforms in UN system including expansion of UNSC
UN Country Team supports SDG implementation and development cooperation
Visit highlights India’s rising importance in international diplomacy
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC General Awareness under International Relations and Organizations, especially United Nations structure, roles, and India’s position in global governance
